Description: Eclipse Soundscapes (ES) assigned a 3-digit identification number that served as a privacy-protecting intermediary that links an AudioMoth device serial number to a data collection location.
If applicable, the group affiliation of the data collector.
Latitude of the AudioMoth device during data collection.
Size KB: Latitude of the AudioMoth device during the observations.

Description: Longitude of the AudioMoth device during data collection.
Description of the type of solar eclipse experienced at the location of data recording (partial eclipse, total eclipse, or annular eclipse) at the location of data collection.
Source: Eclipse Soundscapes' Eclipse Phase Timing Tool
Code Meaning: Total = Local area experienced a total eclipse, Partial = Local area experienced a partial eclipse.

Description: Percentage of the Sun that is blocked by the Moon at the maximum part of the eclipse at the location of data collection.
Source: Eclipse Soundscapes' Eclipse Phase Timing Tool

Description: Variable describing if the data was automatically set by the time chime system or if it needs to be manually set.
If the AudioMoth's clock was not set correctly using the time chime, notes from the Data Collector are provided to allow the start time to be manually set.
Code Meaning: N/A = Not Applicable (AudioMoth time set by Time Chime)
Notes 1: Unlike other time variables, this one does not use the 24 hour time format

Description: The moment when the moon first begins to block the Sun at the location of data collection.
Source: Eclipse Soundscapes' Eclipse Phase Timing Tool

Description: During a total solar eclipse, it is the moment when the moon completely blocks the Sun and totality begins at the location of data collection.
Source: Eclipse Soundscapes' Eclipse Phase Timing Tool

Description: The time at which the most possible amount of the Sun is blocked by the Moon at the location of the data collection.
Source: Eclipse Soundscapes' Eclipse Phase Timing Tool

Description: During a total solar eclipse, it is the moment when the moon no longer completely blocks the Sun and totality ends at the location of data collection.
Source: Eclipse Soundscapes' Eclipse Phase Timing Tool

Description: The moment when the moon no longer blocks any part of the Sun at the location of data collection.
Source: Eclipse Soundscapes' Eclipse Phase Timing Tool

Description: Version code that defines in which year the eclipse was observed and what local eclipse type at the location of data collection
Code Meaning:
{1st digit = year, 2nd digit = Eclipse type (1=Total Solar Eclipse, 9=Annular Solar Eclipse, 0=Partial Solar Eclipse), 3rd digit is unused and in place for future use}
2023.9.0 = Week of October 14, 2023 Annular Eclipse Audio Data, Path of Annularity (Annular Eclipse)
2023.0.0 = Week of October 14, 2023 Annular Eclipse Audio Data, OFF the Path of Annularity (Partial Eclipse)
2024.1.0 = Week of April 8, 2024 Total Solar Eclipse Audio Data, Path of Totality (Total Solar Eclipse)
2024.0.0 =  Week of April 8, 2024 Total Solar Eclipse Audio Data, OFF the Path of Totality (Partial Solar Eclipse)

Description: Denotes the year of the data collection, if the site was used a data analysis site by the ES team, and the Data Collector affiliation if known. These keywords can be used as a sorting function in the Eclipse Soundscapes Comunity Records page under the term subjects in Zenodo.
